signInInformation.title: Changes to Login Process

description.text:
We have merged the login process for EuroLeagueTV and EuroLeague ID – now a single account lets you access both, plus our Fantasy Challenge game.

**If you previously just had a EuroLeagueTV account, please use the forgot password function to activate your new Euroleague ID account.**

If you previously just had a Euroleague ID account, congratulations! You now also have access to a free tier of videos on EuroLeagueTV.\*

**If you are accessing our Fantasy Challenge via our partner's website, please be informed that our partners do not process, collect nor store any user data of participating users as stated in our Privacy Policy**

content.sectionSubtitle: Pick real stars, score real points, and compete with fans across Europe

accordionsInner.content: The EuroLeague Fantasy Challenge is a management game where you act as a General Manager. You have a budget of 100 credits to build a roster of 10 players and 1 head coach. Your players earn points based on their real-life performance (Performance Index Rating) in EuroLeague games. You can make substitutions between "Turns" (game days) within a round and trade players between rounds to improve your team’s value and total score.
Yes. The game is completely free to play. There are no costs associated with creating an account, building teams, or joining leagues.

accordionContent.title: Can I create more than one team?

accordionsInner.content: Yes. Each user is allowed to create and manage up to 20 different teams under a single account. This allows you to experiment with different strategies or player combinations. Note: Only your "primary" team (usually the first one created) may be eligible for certain grand prizes depending on the specific season's terms.

accordionContent.title: Can I create my own league?

accordionsInner.content: Yes. You can create or join Private Leagues to compete against friends, family, or colleagues. When you create a Private League, you will receive a unique code to share with others. You are also automatically entered into the Global League and the league of your favorite EuroLeague club.

Prizes are typically awarded to the top performers in the "Classic" game mode. While they vary by season, they generally include:

- Grand Prize: A VIP trip for two to the EuroLeague Final Four (including flights, luxury hotel, and courtside seats).
- Runner-ups: Official EuroLeague jerseys, Spalding basketballs, and EuroLeague TV season passes.
- Round Winners: Vouchers for the official EuroLeague online store.

content.text:
The EuroLeague Fantasy Challenge Podcast returns with Episode No. 19, as co‑hosts [Javier Gancedo](https://x.com/ganchetto) and [Frankie Sachs](https://x.com/MrEuroleague) of [EuroLeague.net](http://EuroLeague.net) reunite with Sport 24’s [Vasiliki Karamouza](https://x.com/karamouza_vas) to guide managers through a rare Wednesday–Thursday two‑day round. With no guests this week, the trio settles in for a classic strategy session packed with sharp opinions, forward‑thinking planning, and the kind of friendly arguing that long‑time listeners know to expect.

There is plenty to say about the weekend’s domestic cup competitions, which left behind a long list of talking points. The crew breaks down which cup winners might suffer a letdown, which teams gained the biggest boost, and — most importantly — which cup MVPs should be immediate fantasy additions. The result is a rapid‑fire review of breakout performances, rotation changes and confidence surges that could reshape the week ahead.

Among the MVPs was a certain recent signing, which leads to a question already dividing fantasy managers everywhere: Should [Nigel Hayes‑Davis](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/players/nigel-hayes-davis/008962/) be added this week? After Panathinaikos’s star erupted during the Greek Cup stretch, Frankie wonders whether managers should move now — especially before a matchup against Paris — or stay patient and see how things go.

Of course, not every headline is positive, and the panel spends time on the most worrying storyline of the moment: [Sasha Vezenkov](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/players/sasha-vezenkov/003469/)’s back troubles. The group discusses what the injury means for fantasy planning, if managers can afford to hold him, and the teammate who stands to benefit if he’s limited or sidelined.

But the injury chatter doesn’t end there. The hosts also examine the returns of [Lonnie Walker](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/players/lonnie-walker-iv/013403/), [Carlik Jones](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/players/carlik-jones/013369/) and [Shane Larkin](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/players/shane-larkin/007200/), debating how early managers should jump on each player — and which of the three carries the best short‑term upside.

Javi moves things along by mentioning what he likes about [Jordan Nwora](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/players/jordan-nwora/013363/) and [Toko Shengelia](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/players/tornike-shengelia/001133/) this week (hint: it has to do with their opponents). And then comes the moment of the episode: Frankie’s plea for a 4‑credit forward whom “everyone should be considering.” Without giving away the whole pitch, let’s just say the argument is persuasive – and his co-hosts reactions range somewhere between disbelief and admiration. Whether listeners buy in or not, the segment delivers passion.

With big decisions looming in a compact two‑day round, Episode 19 is packed with the tactical detail, data‑driven reasoning and lively debate that define the show. If you’re trying to navigate early pickups, weigh injury uncertainty, or find the hidden value that changes a round, this week’s episode has everything you need.

Tune in, take notes, and get ready – Wednesday gets here fast.

articleMetadata.description: Episode No. 19 features Javier Gancedo, Frankie Sachs and Vasiliki Karamouza tackling a compact Wednesday–Thursday round. The trio debates whether Nigel Hayes‑Davis should be added now versus Paris, breaks down domestic‑cup takeaways (which winners may slump, who gained the most, and which MVPs to prioritize), assesses Sasha Vezenkov’s back situation, and weighs the potential returns of Lonnie Walker, Carlik Jones and Shane Larkin. Plus, Frankie makes a passionate case for a must‑consider 4‑credit forward.

content.text:
With the Super Bowl officially behind us, the EuroLeague Fantasy Challenge Podcast returns to reclaim its rightful place as the week’s main event. Episode No. 18 brings a full house to your screen as co‑hosts [Javier Gancedo](https://x.com/ganchetto) and [Frankie Sachs](https://x.com/MrEuroleague) of [EuroLeague.net](http://EuroLeague.net) team up once again with Sport 24’s [Vasiliki Karamouza](https://x.com/karamouza_vas) — and welcome two special guests: Nikos Tsolakis and Prodromos Boutis, the analytic minds behind the popular Greek‑language *Shot Clock Podcast*. The duo arrives armed with trends and strong opinions, giving this week’s episode a distinctly data‑driven twist.

The expanded panel jumps straight into the issues dominating fantasy discussions right now, beginning with the hottest debate of the week: centers, centers, and more centers. With recent performances and price swings shaking up the position, Javi, Frankie and Vasiliki ask the questions and Nikos and Prodromos weigh in with their own models and theories on how to extract maximum value from the ever‑volatile big‑man group.

The head‑coach slot is always a hot topic, where longtime listeners know one issue always lurks in the shadows: Vasiliki’s supposed head‑coach jinx. This week, concern resurfaced once again — but Frankie isn’t entirely convinced it’s real. In fact, he wonders aloud whether Vasiliki is engaging in some self-sabotage for the benefit of the podcast!

The hosts also spotlight players riding strong momentum into the week, including [Jimmy Clark](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/players/jimmy-clark-iii/013699/), whose recent surge has turned him into a mid‑range gem, and [Andreas Obst](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/players/andreas-obst/004508/), whose shooting form continues to make him an intriguing value option. The conversation expands to team‑wide trends, with looks at the Maccabi and Milan frontcourts — both of which have become puzzle boxes for fantasy managers trying to interpret patterns and rotations.

As always, no Fantasy Challenge episode is complete without an injury recap, and this week the crew runs through the latest news shaping player availability and lineup planning. With several teams dealing with key absences, the hosts help parse which injuries actually create fantasy opportunity and which simply add uncertainty.

Packed with tactical insight, statistical reasoning and plenty of playful banter, Episode No. 18 brings a fresh flavor to the Fantasy Challenge Podcast thanks to Nikos and Prodromos’s guest appearance. Whether you’re reworking your center strategy, trying to dodge the (possibly imaginary) coach jinx, or figuring out which hot hands to trust, this week’s show offers everything you need to prepare for the round ahead.

Tune in, take notes, and get ready to level up your fantasy lineup!

articleMetadata.description: Episode No. 18 of the EuroLeague Fantasy Challenge Podcast welcomes special guests Nikos Tsolakis and Prodromos Boutis from the Greek‑language Shot Clock Podcast, joining co‑hosts Javier Gancedo, Frankie Sachs and Vasiliki Karamouza. The expanded panel delivers a data‑driven discussion on the week’s biggest fantasy dilemmas, with a heavy focus on the chaos at the center position, the strong momentum of Jimmy Clark and Andreas Obst, and the evolving frontcourts in Maccabi and Milan. The group also debates the latest revival of Vasiliki’s head‑coach jinx, breaks down key injuries affecting lineup decisions, and offers strategic guidance heading into Round 28. Packed with insight, humor and fresh perspectives, this episode is a must‑listen for fantasy managers looking to sharpen their edge.

content.text:
It’s time for another packed episode of the EuroLeague Fantasy Challenge Podcast as co‑hosts [Javier Gancedo](https://x.com/ganchetto) and [Frankie Sachs](https://x.com/MrEuroleague) of [EuroLeague.net](http://EuroLeague.net), and Sport 24's [Vasiliki Karamouza](https://x.com/karamouza_vas) return to break down everything fantasy managers need to know ahead of a chaotic week featuring Rounds 26 and 27. No guests this week; just the trio at full strength, plenty of debates, and more than a few laughs.

The crew reveal their winners and losers of the week, including the rare “winner who didn’t even play” – a moment that sparks another round of laughter and a philosophical question about who exactly makes the rules here anyway. They also tackle the biggest strategic challenge of the week: a nine‑game Tuesday slate that forces managers to make nearly all of their decisions at once. Javi, Frankie and Vasiliki outline how to manage early locks and maintain roster flexibility when almost the entire round tips off on the same night.

From there, the focus shifts to the lone Wednesday matchup: [Virtus Bologna vs. ASVEL](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/game-center/2025-26/virtus-bologna-ldlc-asvel-villeurbanne/E2025/260/). With only one game on the final day, targeting the right players becomes crucial. One name rises above the rest: [Braian Angola](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/players/braian-angola/010465/), who the hosts peg as a legitimate Turn 2 differentiator thanks to his price, skillset and mid‑week upside.

Frankie then introduces an unexpected twist: Javi’s very unconventional logic about centers, which even Javi struggles to explain. Frankie tries to apply logic, Vasiliki attempts to mediate, and listeners are invited to choose sides: Are you #TeamJavi or #TeamFrankie – while everyone agrees, of course, that they are #TeamVasiliki.

The show also digs into the risks and upside of Partizan’s [Bruno Fernando](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/players/bruno-fernando/013667/) and [Cam Payne](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/cameron-payne/014213/), whose situations could swing fantasy matchups. Then the trio look ahead with important Round 27 teasers, each offering one early must‑have player managers should already be tracking.

As always, the episode wraps with the “Get Featured, Get Rewarded” segment, where the hosts react to some of the most outrageous listener predictions yet. Some are bold, some are outrageous – all are entertaining.

With all the regular features included – lineup reveals, strategy tips and plenty of banter – this week’s episode is a must‑listen for anyone trying to survive another double week and stay ahead of the curve in the EuroLeague Fantasy Challenge.

content.text:
Episode 16 of the EuroLeague Fantasy Challenge Podcast arrives ahead of a challenging Round 25 with one of everyone’s favorite guests. [Sasha Vezenkov](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/players/sasha-vezenkov/003469/), the 2022-23 MVP and current league leader in PIR, joins the show for his monthly “Sasha’s Corner” segment. The Olympiacos Piraeus star brings fantasy managers rare insight, sharing strategic advice and revealing some of his favorite players at every position.

Podcast co-hosts [Javier Gancedo](https://x.com/ganchetto) and [Frankie Sachs](https://x.com/MrEuroleague) of [EuroLeague.net](http://EuroLeague.net), and Sport 24's [Vasiliki Karamouza](https://x.com/karamouza_vas) also dive into the fallout from Rounds 23 and 24, breaking down the biggest winners, surprises, disappointments, and outright mystery cases of the double‑round week. One of Vasiliki’s preseason predictions even makes an unexpected return — and reminds everyone that not all forecasts age well.

The crew also tackles one of the most stressful parts of the upcoming round: setting rosters on Tuesday, *before* the [Paris Basketball vs. Real Madrid](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/game-center/2025-26/paris-basketball-real-madrid/E2025/241/) showdown. With injuries piling up on different rosters, they debate how fantasy managers can avoid being punished by early lock‑ins and lingering injury reports.

The hosts reveal their teams and discuss which coaches to use – with each of the three taking a different coach into Round 25. From safety picks to high‑risk swings, the debate uncovers what fantasy managers should consider when choosing perhaps the most overlooked spot in the lineup.

As always, the episode features the fan‑driven “Get Featured, Get Rewarded” segment, which this week includes a listener question that sparks a debate: Which Greek team is more likely to make the Final Four? And in one of the lighter moments of the show, listeners ask whether Javi bears a resemblance to a certain EuroLeague‑winning coach.

With MVP‑level insight from Vezenkov, lineup traps to avoid, and strong opinions all around, Episode 16 arms fantasy managers with everything they need to navigate a tricky Round 25.

Get set for a blockbuster double round in the EuroLeague and the EuroLeague Fantasy Challenge! Co-hosts [Javier Gancedo](https://x.com/ganchetto) and [Frankie Sachs](https://x.com/MrEuroleague) of [EuroLeague.net](http://EuroLeague.net), and Sport 24's [Vasiliki Karamouza](https://x.com/karamouza_vas) return for Episode 15 to tackle the madness of nine Tuesday games and their strategic nightmares ahead of Rounds 23 and 24.

As always, the hosts reveal their lineups (no Vezenkov regrets this time!), react to listener feedback in the “Get Featured, Get Rewarded” segment, and drop sleeper gems to dominate the double week. If you’re chasing fantasy glory, this podcast is your cheat code!

content.text:
Get ready for a short but action-packed week in the EuroLeague Fantasy Challenge! Co-hosts [Javier Gancedo](https://eur04.safelinks.protection.outlook.com/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fx.com%2Fganchetto&data=05%7C02%7Cgeoff.gillingham%40euroleague.net%7C1adcd1f47e36414ba0db08de51d1bbf9%7C2b3d64f0329542979f1528435f0a62ce%7C1%7C0%7C639038158424818929%7CUnknown%7CTWFpbGZsb3d8eyJFbXB0eU1hcGkiOnRydWUsIlYiOiIwLjAuMDAwMCIsIlAiOiJXaW4zMiIsIkFOIjoiTWFpbCIsIldUIjoyfQ%3D%3D%7C0%7C%7C%7C&sdata=n224qpkMZ5E3j%2BdMi22Q%2FGSvUxhuBWOKBT8TlBTr124%3D&reserved=0) and [Frankie Sachs](https://eur04.safelinks.protection.outlook.com/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fx.com%2FMrEuroleague&data=05%7C02%7Cgeoff.gillingham%40euroleague.net%7C1adcd1f47e36414ba0db08de51d1bbf9%7C2b3d64f0329542979f1528435f0a62ce%7C1%7C0%7C639038158424833364%7CUnknown%7CTWFpbGZsb3d8eyJFbXB0eU1hcGkiOnRydWUsIlYiOiIwLjAuMDAwMCIsIlAiOiJXaW4zMiIsIkFOIjoiTWFpbCIsIldUIjoyfQ%3D%3D%7C0%7C%7C%7C&sdata=j8aeYF%2Fuz9Vy36ixhwlKUMwkCIVU8ZK3u7mSDbHHAWM%3D&reserved=0) of [EuroLeague.net](http://EuroLeague.net) and Sport 24's [Vasiliki Karamouza](https://eur04.safelinks.protection.outlook.com/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fx.com%2Fkaramouza_vas&data=05%7C02%7Cgeoff.gillingham%40euroleague.net%7C1adcd1f47e36414ba0db08de51d1bbf9%7C2b3d64f0329542979f1528435f0a62ce%7C1%7C0%7C639038158424849675%7CUnknown%7CTWFpbGZsb3d8eyJFbXB0eU1hcGkiOnRydWUsIlYiOiIwLjAuMDAwMCIsIlAiOiJXaW4zMiIsIkFOIjoiTWFpbCIsIldUIjoyfQ%3D%3D%7C0%7C%7C%7C&sdata=P5Q%2BgrlvyRQtqxMaCCj071n3wHnS8TwHWhWjxSyAQyE%3D&reserved=0) return to break down all the key storylines ahead of Round 22 – and this time, Frankie gives a lesson on why mathematics matters in fantasy strategy.

With just one game on Wednesday night – Partizan vs. Olympiacos – the crew dives into how to plan for a condensed schedule and why Saben Lee is a must-own player right now. They debate whether Kenan Kamenjas and Maodo Lo are worth the risk, and analyze the impact of injuries to Bonzie Colson, Nigel Williams-Goss, Monte Morris, and Shake Milton on fantasy rosters.

Plus, the hosts share their thoughts on last week’s biggest transactions, including Nando De Colo, Tyrique Jones, Tonye Jekiri, and Chris Silva, and explain why centers are suddenly aplenty again – with Richaun Holmes at the top of that list. 

 As always, Javi, Frankie and Vasiliki reveal their own fantasy teams (spoiler: Javi is not rolling with Sasha Vezenkov!) and react to comments from listeners in the “Get Featured, Get Rewarded” segment. If you love the EuroLeague and want to dominate your fantasy league, this is the podcast for you!

content.text:
It's time for Episode 5! There is little time to set your EuroLeague Fantasy Challenge lineups for Round 6 this week with eight games on Tuesday night! So tune in now to hear from [Frankie Sachs](https://eur04.safelinks.protection.outlook.com/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fx.com%2FMrEuroleague&data=05%7C02%7Cgeoff.gillingham%40euroleague.net%7C8f23c17ba6e24a938a4c08ddff3a0ce8%7C2b3d64f0329542979f1528435f0a62ce%7C1%7C0%7C638947346960894891%7CUnknown%7CTWFpbGZsb3d8eyJFbXB0eU1hcGkiOnRydWUsIlYiOiIwLjAuMDAwMCIsIlAiOiJXaW4zMiIsIkFOIjoiTWFpbCIsIldUIjoyfQ%3D%3D%7C0%7C%7C%7C&sdata=qhPt96t3ngeIaZ%2FSU%2FA67vi5euxZ5OVn9QfMhhj343M%3D&reserved=0) of [EuroLeague.net](http://EuroLeague.net), Sport 24's [Vasiliki Karamouza](https://eur04.safelinks.protection.outlook.com/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fx.com%2Fkaramouza_vas&data=05%7C02%7Cgeoff.gillingham%40euroleague.net%7C8f23c17ba6e24a938a4c08ddff3a0ce8%7C2b3d64f0329542979f1528435f0a62ce%7C1%7C0%7C638947346960937569%7CUnknown%7CTWFpbGZsb3d8eyJFbXB0eU1hcGkiOnRydWUsIlYiOiIwLjAuMDAwMCIsIlAiOiJXaW4zMiIsIkFOIjoiTWFpbCIsIldUIjoyfQ%3D%3D%7C0%7C%7C%7C&sdata=RuVg%2FRj%2BbH%2FiJbU%2BYkpXVo6vX%2B39lXvvomI9gNtMJLs%3D&reserved=0) and special guard and expert Arale Weisberg for a special Eastern Mediterranean Episode.

The three of them take you through the latest injury news, from the questions surrounding Partizan Mozzart Bet Belgrade and Crvena Zvezda Meridianbet Belgrade, to how to handle Nigel Williams-Goss absence for Zalgiris Kaunas and what to make of Kendrick Nunn and Juancho Hernangomez sitting out Panathinaikos AKTOR Athens’s Greek league win on Sunday.

They also discuss strategy tips for both ends of the two-round week. Additional topics covered were how old Nando De Colo really is, the first-half scoring ability of Lonnie Walker, whether Donatas Motiejunas is the best-priced center in fantasy and if Vasiliki can be trusted to choose head coaches. As a bonus topic, Frankie is asked if Nadir Hifi iffy!

As always, Frankie, Vasiliki and Arale, reveal their teams for Round 7 and react to questions sent in by Fantasy Podcast viewers. Enjoy this episode and best of luck to our fantasy friends in this week's games.

content.text:
A new feature is unveiled in Episode No. 3 of the third season of the EuroLeague Fantasy Challenge Podcast as All-EuroLeague forward [Sasha Vezenkov](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/players/sasha-vezenkov/003469/) delivers his fantasy tips in Sasha's Corner. The [Olympiacos Piraeus](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/teams/olympiacos-piraeus/oly/) star has committed to filming this segment every month and sharing his insights as a committed fantasy player.

The rest of your favorite fantasy content is also in the episode, with co-hosts [Javier Gancedo](https://eur04.safelinks.protection.outlook.com/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fx.com%2Fganchetto&data=05%7C02%7Cgeoff.gillingham%40euroleague.net%7C8f23c17ba6e24a938a4c08ddff3a0ce8%7C2b3d64f0329542979f1528435f0a62ce%7C1%7C0%7C638947346960858240%7CUnknown%7CTWFpbGZsb3d8eyJFbXB0eU1hcGkiOnRydWUsIlYiOiIwLjAuMDAwMCIsIlAiOiJXaW4zMiIsIkFOIjoiTWFpbCIsIldUIjoyfQ%3D%3D%7C0%7C%7C%7C&sdata=5ichQZ08%2B4%2BAejvWrkbiWc2ckcGKFSoR6Wz%2Fl3MdtWE%3D&reserved=0) and [Frankie Sachs](https://eur04.safelinks.protection.outlook.com/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fx.com%2FMrEuroleague&data=05%7C02%7Cgeoff.gillingham%40euroleague.net%7C8f23c17ba6e24a938a4c08ddff3a0ce8%7C2b3d64f0329542979f1528435f0a62ce%7C1%7C0%7C638947346960894891%7CUnknown%7CTWFpbGZsb3d8eyJFbXB0eU1hcGkiOnRydWUsIlYiOiIwLjAuMDAwMCIsIlAiOiJXaW4zMiIsIkFOIjoiTWFpbCIsIldUIjoyfQ%3D%3D%7C0%7C%7C%7C&sdata=qhPt96t3ngeIaZ%2FSU%2FA67vi5euxZ5OVn9QfMhhj343M%3D&reserved=0) of [EuroLeague.net](http://EuroLeague.net) and Sport 24's [Vasiliki Karamouza](https://eur04.safelinks.protection.outlook.com/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fx.com%2Fkaramouza_vas&data=05%7C02%7Cgeoff.gillingham%40euroleague.net%7C8f23c17ba6e24a938a4c08ddff3a0ce8%7C2b3d64f0329542979f1528435f0a62ce%7C1%7C0%7C638947346960937569%7CUnknown%7CTWFpbGZsb3d8eyJFbXB0eU1hcGkiOnRydWUsIlYiOiIwLjAuMDAwMCIsIlAiOiJXaW4zMiIsIkFOIjoiTWFpbCIsIldUIjoyfQ%3D%3D%7C0%7C%7C%7C&sdata=RuVg%2FRj%2BbH%2FiJbU%2BYkpXVo6vX%2B39lXvvomI9gNtMJLs%3D&reserved=0) discussing the latest EuroLeague injuries and news as well as strategies for Rounds 4 and 5 this week. 

The trio talks about Zalgiris Kaunas's red-hot start, the backcourt situations at FC Bayern Munich and Fenerbahce Beko Istanbul, and Dan Oturu's emergence as a top-choice center. Javi, Frankie and Vasiliki, of course, reveal their teams for Round 4 as well and react to questions sent in by Fantasy Podcast viewers. Enjoy this episode and best of luck to our fantasy friends in this week's games.

content.text:
To Nunn or not to Nunn? That is one of the biggest questions to be answered in episode No. 29 of the EuroLeague Fantasy Challenge podcast. The gang is back together and co-hosts [Javier Gancedo](https://eur04.safelinks.protection.outlook.com/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fx.com%2Fganchetto&data=05%7C02%7Cigor.petrinovic%40euroleague.net%7Cd31497b64394488cb00c08dd8bb37e6b%7C2b3d64f0329542979f1528435f0a62ce%7C1%7C0%7C638820325223923622%7CUnknown%7CTWFpbGZsb3d8eyJFbXB0eU1hcGkiOnRydWUsIlYiOiIwLjAuMDAwMCIsIlAiOiJXaW4zMiIsIkFOIjoiTWFpbCIsIldUIjoyfQ%3D%3D%7C0%7C%7C%7C&sdata=5tNH%2BQWNoYSgCFd0ChRRhky4K9vFi3ebTNQT8Dwp1zQ%3D&reserved=0) and [Frankie Sachs](https://eur04.safelinks.protection.outlook.com/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fx.com%2FMrEuroleague&data=05%7C02%7Cigor.petrinovic%40euroleague.net%7Cd31497b64394488cb00c08dd8bb37e6b%7C2b3d64f0329542979f1528435f0a62ce%7C1%7C0%7C638820325223944385%7CUnknown%7CTWFpbGZsb3d8eyJFbXB0eU1hcGkiOnRydWUsIlYiOiIwLjAuMDAwMCIsIlAiOiJXaW4zMiIsIkFOIjoiTWFpbCIsIldUIjoyfQ%3D%3D%7C0%7C%7C%7C&sdata=IGy%2BEGWZkAc794Ikvk2coJH9JO1ZniZQzAvgXeHl4GY%3D&reserved=0) of [EuroLeague.net](http://EuroLeague.net) and Sport 24's [Vasiliki Karamouza](https://eur04.safelinks.protection.outlook.com/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fx.com%2Fkaramouza_vas&data=05%7C02%7Cigor.petrinovic%40euroleague.net%7Cd31497b64394488cb00c08dd8bb37e6b%7C2b3d64f0329542979f1528435f0a62ce%7C1%7C0%7C638820325223955177%7CUnknown%7CTWFpbGZsb3d8eyJFbXB0eU1hcGkiOnRydWUsIlYiOiIwLjAuMDAwMCIsIlAiOiJXaW4zMiIsIkFOIjoiTWFpbCIsIldUIjoyfQ%3D%3D%7C0%7C%7C%7C&sdata=iWMdkeGl8BL43%2FwZ10BoQVxeAiWr2tTRDPAml3GIwl8%3D&reserved=0) debate – and disagree – on that topic and more!

If not Nunn, who should your MVP be this week? Is there a center who stands out? How many FC Barcelona players are too many on a roster? What’s the better shorthand for Mike James: 5K or MJ55? Javi, Frankie and Vasiliki take a crack at all of these topics.

With only two Game 5s to be played – and both on Tuesday night, there is little room for error when making your teams. So be sure to listen to what the gang has to say this week. It just may be what puts you over the top. And as always, good luck to our fantasy friends!

content.text:
The trio of EuroLeague Fantasy Challenge Podcast co-hosts are back together again with [Javier Gancedo](https://eur04.safelinks.protection.outlook.com/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fx.com%2Fganchetto&data=05%7C02%7Cigor.petrinovic%40euroleague.net%7C78f411fe745c432da56508dd7b56c320%7C2b3d64f0329542979f1528435f0a62ce%7C1%7C0%7C638802334749751491%7CUnknown%7CTWFpbGZsb3d8eyJFbXB0eU1hcGkiOnRydWUsIlYiOiIwLjAuMDAwMCIsIlAiOiJXaW4zMiIsIkFOIjoiTWFpbCIsIldUIjoyfQ%3D%3D%7C0%7C%7C%7C&sdata=8DKh%2BlF4corkMBAdaOaCieST%2BUgrtvMtJae4Zk09uoI%3D&reserved=0) making his triumphant return alongside [Vasiliki Karamouza](https://eur04.safelinks.protection.outlook.com/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fx.com%2Fkaramouza_vas&data=05%7C02%7Cigor.petrinovic%40euroleague.net%7C78f411fe745c432da56508dd7b56c320%7C2b3d64f0329542979f1528435f0a62ce%7C1%7C0%7C638802334749767493%7CUnknown%7CTWFpbGZsb3d8eyJFbXB0eU1hcGkiOnRydWUsIlYiOiIwLjAuMDAwMCIsIlAiOiJXaW4zMiIsIkFOIjoiTWFpbCIsIldUIjoyfQ%3D%3D%7C0%7C%7C%7C&sdata=3sAFU21%2Fno29DHV7IoXkiDi3IBRJLJ32pGJ%2FpBP58EM%3D&reserved=0) and [Frankie Sachs](https://eur04.safelinks.protection.outlook.com/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fx.com%2FMrEuroleague&data=05%7C02%7Cigor.petrinovic%40euroleague.net%7C78f411fe745c432da56508dd7b56c320%7C2b3d64f0329542979f1528435f0a62ce%7C1%7C0%7C638802334749778256%7CUnknown%7CTWFpbGZsb3d8eyJFbXB0eU1hcGkiOnRydWUsIlYiOiIwLjAuMDAwMCIsIlAiOiJXaW4zMiIsIkFOIjoiTWFpbCIsIldUIjoyfQ%3D%3D%7C0%7C%7C%7C&sdata=t830C3BcgSc7yJU4%2BG1rjayHdVh6A7tj%2BY8%2F%2F9U1B0M%3D&reserved=0). There is only main topic of conversation this week: The Turkish Airlines EuroLeague Play-In Showdown.

The trio discusses the best strategy to employ for Tuesday's two games and what risks are worth taking. There is a lot of overlap between the three of them with the likes of TJ Shorts, Walter Tavares and Filip Petrusev universally recommended. In fact, Frankie and Javi chose identical starting lineups!

Time is of the essence with the Play-In Showdown tipping on Tuesday when FC Bayern Munich hosts Crvena Zvezda Meridianbet Belgrade at 20:30 CEST, so make sure to have your lineups finalized by then. And, as always, good luck, fantasy fans.

content.text:
Co-hosts [Vasiliki Karamouza](https://eur04.safelinks.protection.outlook.com/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fx.com%2Fkaramouza_vas&data=05%7C02%7Cigor.petrinovic%40euroleague.net%7C232fd770c6fd43aba8bf08dd76bd7c72%7C2b3d64f0329542979f1528435f0a62ce%7C1%7C0%7C638797278386340010%7CUnknown%7CTWFpbGZsb3d8eyJFbXB0eU1hcGkiOnRydWUsIlYiOiIwLjAuMDAwMCIsIlAiOiJXaW4zMiIsIkFOIjoiTWFpbCIsIldUIjoyfQ%3D%3D%7C0%7C%7C%7C&sdata=dsdEhd8BdcLOVkgjYgEnm%2FaAN8X3hkXiZAtoh58cayU%3D&reserved=0) and [Frankie Sachs](https://eur04.safelinks.protection.outlook.com/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fx.com%2FMrEuroleague&data=05%7C02%7Cigor.petrinovic%40euroleague.net%7C232fd770c6fd43aba8bf08dd76bd7c72%7C2b3d64f0329542979f1528435f0a62ce%7C1%7C0%7C638797278386384970%7CUnknown%7CTWFpbGZsb3d8eyJFbXB0eU1hcGkiOnRydWUsIlYiOiIwLjAuMDAwMCIsIlAiOiJXaW4zMiIsIkFOIjoiTWFpbCIsIldUIjoyfQ%3D%3D%7C0%7C%7C%7C&sdata=rHOctLZURW1A1P1rIVNjW4ML98uYkflto0fka96%2F0EQ%3D&reserved=0) are on their own again for Episode No. 26 of the EuroLeague Fantasy Challenge Podcast, with [Javier Gancedo](https://eur04.safelinks.protection.outlook.com/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fx.com%2Fganchetto&data=05%7C02%7Cigor.petrinovic%40euroleague.net%7C232fd770c6fd43aba8bf08dd76bd7c72%7C2b3d64f0329542979f1528435f0a62ce%7C1%7C0%7C638797278386402935%7CUnknown%7CTWFpbGZsb3d8eyJFbXB0eU1hcGkiOnRydWUsIlYiOiIwLjAuMDAwMCIsIlAiOiJXaW4zMiIsIkFOIjoiTWFpbCIsIldUIjoyfQ%3D%3D%7C0%7C%7C%7C&sdata=WaNw8X6bsIFI4uZgRuDy3fJio0qNKM3vXCyVxHwsyQA%3D&reserved=0) in Bulgaria for the BKT EuroCup Finals. And there is plenty to discuss ahead of Round 34, which marks the final time there will be a full lineup of games in the EuroLeague Fantasy Challenge.

Frankie starts by explaining why he is staying away from players whose teams have already been eliminated and then issues a heartfelt thank you to three of his favorite players from the season who won't suit up for him again: [Theo Maledon](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/players/theo-maledon/007027/), [Roman Sorkin](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/players/roman-sorkin/011219/) and [David McCormack](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/players/david-mccormack/013386/). Vasiliki continues with an analysis of the Olympiacos Piraeus backcourt situation and which players she sees on the rise.

While both Frankie and Vasiliki see eye to eye on their coaches and several players for Round 34, it’s interesting to hear why they move in different directions on the rest of the roster. This week the Round begins at 20:00 CEST with games tipping off simultaneously in Germany and France, so make sure to have your lineups finalized by then. And, as always, good luck, fantasy fans.

With [Javier Gancedo](https://eur04.safelinks.protection.outlook.com/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fx.com%2Fganchetto&data=05%7C02%7Cgeoff.gillingham%40euroleague.net%7Cd5538e3885f74e148dae08dd6ad32641%7C2b3d64f0329542979f1528435f0a62ce%7C1%7C0%7C638784177282492773%7CUnknown%7CTWFpbGZsb3d8eyJFbXB0eU1hcGkiOnRydWUsIlYiOiIwLjAuMDAwMCIsIlAiOiJXaW4zMiIsIkFOIjoiTWFpbCIsIldUIjoyfQ%3D%3D%7C0%7C%7C%7C&sdata=cqHvS%2Fo%2BuTedtCFtxT8V7ee%2FvopzX1kyjXZD0oLgatI%3D&reserved=0) out partying, co-hosts [Vasiliki Karamouza](https://x.com/karamouza_vas) and [Frankie Sachs](https://eur04.safelinks.protection.outlook.com/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fx.com%2FMrEuroleague&data=05%7C02%7Cgeoff.gillingham%40euroleague.net%7Cd5538e3885f74e148dae08dd6ad32641%7C2b3d64f0329542979f1528435f0a62ce%7C1%7C0%7C638784177282517913%7CUnknown%7CTWFpbGZsb3d8eyJFbXB0eU1hcGkiOnRydWUsIlYiOiIwLjAuMDAwMCIsIlAiOiJXaW4zMiIsIkFOIjoiTWFpbCIsIldUIjoyfQ%3D%3D%7C0%7C%7C%7C&sdata=jYTqaHWOmXpdpUJew4Ihsp615bj%2Fc8Hyn4kpubedxxM%3D&reserved=0) teamed up for Episode No. 25 of the EuroLeague Fantasy Challenge Podcast. The show starts with a bang as Vassiliki breaks some news and Frankie reveals the details of their private WhatsApp messages last week!

Then it's on to the Fantasy Challenge and detailed discussions about the merits of [FC Barcelona](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/teams/fc-barcelona/roster/bar/?season=2024-25) players (Frankie has three on his team!) and [Fenerbahce Beko Istanbul](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/teams/fenerbahce-beko-istanbul/roster/ulk/?season=2024-25) players ahead of their Wednesday night game. Vassiliki is stuck in a dilemma between [Kendrick Nunn](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/players/kendrick-nunn/012774/) and [Mike James](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/players/mike-james/005985/) and Frankie has mixed feelings about [Juancho Hernangomez](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/players/juancho-hernangomez/012712/).

Vasiliki and Frankie go through their choices for Round 33 as well as their captains, including how many points their Wednesday and Thursday captains needs to score to before deciding whether to name [Sasha Vezenkov](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/players/sasha-vezenkov/003469/) (in a matchup against [ALBA Berlin](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/teams/alba-berlin/roster/ber/?season=2024-25)) as captain on Friday. Don’t get caught off-guard by the Wednesday showdown in Istanbul between Fenerbahce and Barcelona, so make sure to set your lineups before they tip off at 19:45 CET. And, as always, good luck, fantasy fans.

content.text:
Vasiliki Karamouza makes her long-awaited return to the EuroLeague Fantasy Challenge Podcast for Episode No. 24. Reunited with co-hosts [Javier Gancedo](https://eur04.safelinks.protection.outlook.com/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fx.com%2Fganchetto&data=05%7C02%7Cgeoff.gillingham%40euroleague.net%7Cd5538e3885f74e148dae08dd6ad32641%7C2b3d64f0329542979f1528435f0a62ce%7C1%7C0%7C638784177282492773%7CUnknown%7CTWFpbGZsb3d8eyJFbXB0eU1hcGkiOnRydWUsIlYiOiIwLjAuMDAwMCIsIlAiOiJXaW4zMiIsIkFOIjoiTWFpbCIsIldUIjoyfQ%3D%3D%7C0%7C%7C%7C&sdata=cqHvS%2Fo%2BuTedtCFtxT8V7ee%2FvopzX1kyjXZD0oLgatI%3D&reserved=0) and [Frankie Sachs](https://eur04.safelinks.protection.outlook.com/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fx.com%2FMrEuroleague&data=05%7C02%7Cgeoff.gillingham%40euroleague.net%7Cd5538e3885f74e148dae08dd6ad32641%7C2b3d64f0329542979f1528435f0a62ce%7C1%7C0%7C638784177282517913%7CUnknown%7CTWFpbGZsb3d8eyJFbXB0eU1hcGkiOnRydWUsIlYiOiIwLjAuMDAwMCIsIlAiOiJXaW4zMiIsIkFOIjoiTWFpbCIsIldUIjoyfQ%3D%3D%7C0%7C%7C%7C&sdata=jYTqaHWOmXpdpUJew4Ihsp615bj%2Fc8Hyn4kpubedxxM%3D&reserved=0) of [Euroleague.net](http://Euroleague.net), the gang discusses what worked – and didn't in Round 30 – as well as who's hot, who's hurt and, of course, who they believe in for Rounds 31 and 32.

Once again centers were a hot topic with [Daniel Theis](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/players/daniel-theis/004132/) of AS Monaco, [Walter Tavares](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/players/walter-tavares/005791/) of Real Madrid and [Moustapha Fall](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/players/moustapha-fall/008811/) of Olympiacos Piraeus among the names mentioned. Crvena Zvezda Meridianbet Belgrade big man [Filip Petrusev](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/filip-petrusev/011194/) was also universally recommended.  

The gang also discussed which teams have advantages you may want to exploit for the second set of games this week. Javi, Frankie and Vasiliki share their lineups for Round 31, including who are their choices for captain (#VezforPrez), too. Time is of the essence in this double round, with the action beginning at 18:45 CET on Tuesday when Fenerbahce Beko Istanbul hosts Paris Basketball, so be sure to have your lineups set before then! Good luck, fantasy fans.

content.text:
Episode No. 23 of the EuroLeague Fantasy Challenge Podcast is destined to be a great one in honor of greats of the game of basketball like Michael Jordan and Sergio Llull who have donned that number in their legendary career. Regular co-hosts [Javier Gancedo](https://eur04.safelinks.protection.outlook.com/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fx.com%2Fganchetto&data=05%7C02%7Cgeoff.gillingham%40euroleague.net%7Cff2a7b4fdb8942bff95208dd654005c4%7C2b3d64f0329542979f1528435f0a62ce%7C1%7C0%7C638778047824882217%7CUnknown%7CTWFpbGZsb3d8eyJFbXB0eU1hcGkiOnRydWUsIlYiOiIwLjAuMDAwMCIsIlAiOiJXaW4zMiIsIkFOIjoiTWFpbCIsIldUIjoyfQ%3D%3D%7C0%7C%7C%7C&sdata=sVGmeHZVSTZXEoK1dfvl8YTvEa6Toa04F3Qok%2FuiEr8%3D&reserved=0) and [Frankie Sachs](https://eur04.safelinks.protection.outlook.com/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fx.com%2FMrEuroleague&data=05%7C02%7Cgeoff.gillingham%40euroleague.net%7Cff2a7b4fdb8942bff95208dd654005c4%7C2b3d64f0329542979f1528435f0a62ce%7C1%7C0%7C638778047824901897%7CUnknown%7CTWFpbGZsb3d8eyJFbXB0eU1hcGkiOnRydWUsIlYiOiIwLjAuMDAwMCIsIlAiOiJXaW4zMiIsIkFOIjoiTWFpbCIsIldUIjoyfQ%3D%3D%7C0%7C%7C%7C&sdata=OsbA2cH%2Fonzp6wHTixlMtT0Z7Z56BNvCYzb4CalbbaI%3D&reserved=0) of [Euroleague.net](http://Euroleague.net) joined forced again this week, with Vasiliki Karamouza experiencing technical difficulties.

The name of the fantasy game for Round 30 is centers, with [Joel Bolomboy](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/players/joel-bolomboy/009035/) of Crvena Zvezda Meridianbet Belgrade sidelined and [Daniel Theis](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/players/daniel-theis/004132/) of AS Monaco questionable. Moreover, a Tuesday night fixture between Paris Basketball and EA7 Emporio Armani Milan means that a decision must be made by Tuesday evening, so waiting on player updates can be risky.

Javi and Frankie discussed alternatives at center as well as how to build your teams in this three-turn round. If you are – like Frankie – among those who lost both of your centers to injuries, you have come to the right place for help! See what moves Javi and Frankie advise – as well as what they actually do – this week. And make sure your lineups are set by 20:30 CET on Tuesday when Paris vs. Milan tips off in the French capital. Good luck, fantasy fans!

Get ready for the games of Rounds 25 and 26 with two of your favorite fantasy experts.

Amon the topics discussed this week were FC Bayern Munich’s frontline after the reported injury to Devin Booker, which underpriced players they have their eyes on (Roman Sorkin? P.J. Dozier?) and the difficulty in settling on coaches in Round 25 (Frankie went with Ioannis Sfairopoulos, Vasiliki chose Andrea Trinchieri).

Check out the moves Frankie and Vasiliki have made ahead of Round 25, including who they have as captains – and what performance is needed from Turn 1 captains before turning to Sasha Vezenkov in Turn 2! This week’s action tips off on Tuesday at 18:30 CET when Real Madrid meets Anadolu Efes Istanbul, so be sure to finalize your rosters by then. Good luck, fantasy fans!

These questions and many more were debated by the trio of fantasy aficionados ahead of Round 23. content.text:
Check out the latest episode of the EuroLeague Fantasy Challenge Podcast to prepare your fantasy teams for Round 20. Co-hosts [Javier Gancedo](https://x.com/ganchetto) and [Frankie Sachs](https://x.com/MrEuroleague) of [Euroleague.net](http://Euroleague.net) and [Vasiliki Karamouza](https://x.com/karamouza_vas) of Sport 24 celebrate some of their successes last week and look ahead to a new round of Turkish Airlines EuroLeague games.

The trio discussed the center situation at [Panathinaikos AKTOR Athens](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/teams/panathinaikos-aktor-athens/pan/) (is Round 19 MVP [Omer Yurtseven](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/omer-yurtseven/005353/) the answer?), the backcourt of [Zalgiris Kaunas](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/teams/zalgiris-kaunas/zal/) (should you ride the [Sylvain Francisco](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/players/sylvain-francisco/007975/) wave?) and who you should add from [Fenerbahce Beko Istanbul](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/teams/fenerbahce-beko-istanbul/ulk/) (is [Nigel Hayes-Davis](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/nigel-hayes-davis/008962/) due for another huge game against ALBA?), among many other topics.

Check out the moves Javi, Frankie and Vasiliki have made ahead of Round 20, including who they have as captains. This week’s action tips off on Thursday at 18:45 CET in Istanbul with the duel between Fenerbahce and ALBA, so make sure to finalize your rosters by then. Good luck, fantasy fans!

Check out the teams Javi, Frankie and Vasiliki plan to use in Round 18, which begins at 19:00 CET on Thursday when games tip off simultaneously in Berlin and Monaco. Good luck fantasy fans!

content.text:
Another star of the Turkish Airlines EuroLeague has decided to jump into the fantasy fray with [Bonzie Colson](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/players/bonzie-colson/009783/) of [Fenerbahce Beko Istanbul](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/teams/fenerbahce-beko-istanbul/ulk/) coming aboard as the latest guest on the EuroLeague Fantasy Challenge Podcast. The original co-hosts, [Javier Gancedo](https://x.com/ganchetto) and [Frankie Sachs](https://x.com/MrEuroleague) of [Euroleague.net](http://Euroleague.net), share the episode with Colson, with [Vasiliki Karamouza](https://x.com/karamouza_vas) of Sport 24 unavailable to join this week.

Colson is big on dissecting weekly matchups to maximize fantasy value and shared that though process on the podcast. He also spoke about [Nigel Hayes-Davis](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/nigel-hayes-davis/008962/)’s greatness, [Lonnie Walker](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/players/lonnie-walker-iv/013403/)’s fantastic start to the season and his former teammate at [Maccabi Playtika Tel Aviv](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/teams/maccabi-playtika-tel-aviv/tel/), [Roman Sorkin](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/roman-sorkin/011219/).  

Check out Bonzie’s team and the changes Javi and Frankie are making to their teams as you prepare your fantasy team for Round 15, which begins at 18:45 CET on Thursday in Istanbul when Colson and Fenerbahce take on [Mike James](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/mike-james/005985/) and [AS Monaco](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/teams/as-monaco/mco/).

content.text:
Episode No. 10 of the EuroLeague Fantasy Challenge Podcast sees a change to the cast with [Javier Gancedo](https://x.com/ganchetto) away. So to fill in those big shoes, co-hosts [Frankie Sachs](https://x.com/MrEuroleague) of [Euroleague.net](http://Euroleague.net) and [Vasiliki Karamouza](https://x.com/karamouza_vas) of Sport 24 bring in [Virtus Segafredo Bologna](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/teams/virtus-segafredo-bologna/vir/) star and Olympic silver medalist [Isaia Cordinier](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/isaia-cordinier/009549/) to talk EuroLeague and fantasy hoops.

Before getting into the nitty-gritty of their [EuroLeague Fantasy Challenge](https://eur04.safelinks.protection.outlook.com/?url=https%3A%2F%2Feuroleaguefantasy.euroleaguebasketball.net%2F10&data=05%7C02%7Cgeoff.gillingham%40euroleague.net%7C0811b21f4e94405fce1908dd13030770%7C2b3d64f0329542979f1528435f0a62ce%7C1%7C0%7C638687625911097302%7CUnknown%7CTWFpbGZsb3d8eyJFbXB0eU1hcGkiOnRydWUsIlYiOiIwLjAuMDAwMCIsIlAiOiJXaW4zMiIsIkFOIjoiTWFpbCIsIldUIjoyfQ%3D%3D%7C0%7C%7C%7C&sdata=XQi0EE05ooN0xNXXX1fCDXXtDgaCiAScCe3M98GEGc0%3D&reserved=0) teams, Cordinier talks about Virtus’s season; his former teammates Theo Maledon, Evan Fournier and Nico Mannion; November MVP TJ Shorts; and the advantages Virtus has heading into this week’s two rounds of games. Then it’s time to share his team and see how many of Cordinier's compatriots make it onto his fantasy roster. Finally, see whether Cordinier approves of the roster changes Frankie and Vasiliki made heading into Round 13.

Listen now for some thoughts and ideas that could help your team before Round 13, which tips off at 18:30 CET on Tuesday when Anadolu Efes Istanbul hosts FC Bayern Munich, and even later in the week when preparing for Round 14.

That means EuroLeague Fantasy Challenge Podcast co-hosts [Javier Gancedo](https://x.com/ganchetto) and [Frankie Sachs](https://x.com/MrEuroleague) of [Euroleague.net](http://Euroleague.net) and Sport 24's [Vasiliki Karamouza](https://x.com/karamouza_vas) will share their tips and insights on the best bargains and must-own players as we look ahead to Round 5 of the 2024-25 Turkish Airlines EuroLeague Regular Season – and beyond.

How does the Partizan Mozzart Bet Belgrade vs. Virtus Segafredo Bologna game on Wednesday affect things? Isaac Bonga – yea or nay? Theo Maledon or Mike James- or both? Does the gang agree on the best options at center? Listen now to best prepare yourself for this week’s action.

content.text:
The gang returns for the latest edition of the EuroLeague Fantasy Challenge Podcast! [Javier Gancedo](https://x.com/ganchetto) and [Frankie Sachs](https://x.com/MrEuroleague) of [Euroleague.net](http://Euroleague.net) are joined by Sport 24's [Vasiliki Karamouza](https://x.com/karamouza_vas) to debate the best strategies and players ahead for Round 3 of the 2024-25 Turkish Airlines EuroLeague Regular Season – and how it pertains to Round 4.

Among the topics they discussed are why it is important to have FC Barcelona and/or AS Monaco players, whether Vasiliki is a genius or a maniac for her unorthodox thinking about the centers on her team, and, of course, which players they have their eyes on.  

Time is of the essence, so listen now to best prepare yourself for this week’s action. And you just may be able to win a special prize! The details are in the episode.

content.text:
The news that everybody had been waiting for has finally arrived: [Euroleague.net](http://Euroleague.net)'s Javier Gancedo is back on the EuroLeague Fantasy Challenge Podcast! 

Alongside [Euroleague.net](http://Euroleague.net) colleague Frankie Sachs and Sport 24's Vasiliki Karamouza, the three fantasy experts offer their advice to fantasy managers as we head into Round 2 of the 2024-25 Turkish Airlines EuroLeague season.

One of the main talking points surrounds the success that Maccabi Playtika Tel Aviv guard Tamir Blatt last week as he achieved 39.6 fantasy points, more than anybody else. 

The three fantasy gurus also discuss the importance of this three-turn week, with fantasy managers encouraged to listen to the advice of Javier, Frankie and Vasiliki in order to maximize their potential points tally across the games on Wednesday, Thursday and Friday.

content.text:
The EuroLeague Fantasy Challenge Podcast is back for Season 2 with production upgrades and a new co-host! [Vasiliki Karamouza](https://x.com/karamouza_vas) of Sport 24 in Greece will join [Frankie Sachs](https://x.com/MrEuroleague) and [Javier Gancedo](https://x.com/ganchetto) of [Euroleague.net](http://Euroleague.net) as a regular host to share her insights on everyone’s favorite Turkish Airlines EuroLeague companion contest.

Vasiliki and Frankie share the mic in Episode 1 to detail the new rule changes this season, reveal how to join the podcast’s private fantasy league and, of course, give some tips on how to build your rosters. What obstacle is Frankie facing in Round 1? Who are Vasiliki’s must-have players? Get the answers to these questions and more by listening to the podcast now.
